name: Berry–Tabor Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the ensemble of all Hamiltonian eigenvalue distributions across integrable and chaotic regimes

VINIT.justification: void state before any perturbation or measurement; the pristine Hamiltonian before chaos emerges

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor containing all possible eigenvalue distributions; the hermetic closure of all regimes from integrable to fully chaotic

AFWD.justification: forward morphism: as perturbation grows, system evolves from integrable toward chaotic; directed transformation toward chaos

AREV.justification: reverse morphism: descent back toward integrability; contravariant flow reducing chaos

CLINK.justification: composition of eigenvalue transformations across perturbation steps; chaining of level-spacing statistics

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference: the spectrum recognizes itself through its own statistical signature; identity of the distribution under its own measure

FSPLIT.chosen_element: bifurcation into integrable vs. chaotic regimes
FSPLIT.justification: one spectral ensemble branches into two distinct statistical behaviors: Poisson (integrable) and Wigner-Dyson (chaotic)

FFUSE.justification: the intermediate perturbation regime where both statistics coexist; fusing Poisson and Wigner-Dyson branches recovers the full spectral ensemble

EVALT.justification: affirmative: level spacing follows Wigner-Dyson statistics; true positive identification of chaos

EVALF.justification: negative: level spacing follows Poisson statistics; absence of spectral rigidity indicates integrability

ENGAGR.justification: both Poisson and Wigner-Dyson statistics held simultaneously in the crossover; neither pure integrable nor pure chaotic, a genuine paradice

IFIX.justification: permanent append-only record: once eigenvalues are measured and fixed in the spectrum, they cannot be undone; irreversible fixation of quantum state

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: bifurcation into integrable vs. chaotic regimes
phase_2.split_input: unperturbed spectral ensemble with perturbation parameter λ
phase_2.fuse_result: complete spectral ensemble across all perturbation strengths, recovering the original input distribution

phase_3.void_description: 00 — uninitialized: no perturbation applied, no spectral measurement taken, system in superposition of all possible Hamiltonians
phase_3.true_description: 01 — chaotic regime: level spacing exhibits Wigner-Dyson statistics, spectral rigidity confirmed, quantum chaos verified
phase_3.false_description: 10 — integrable regime: level spacing exhibits Poisson statistics, no spectral correlations, integrability confirmed
phase_3.both_description: 11 — transition paradice: crossover region where both Poisson and Wigner-Dyson statistics coexist simultaneously, neither pure state dominant

steps.domain_action: genesis of unperturbed integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with no perturbation
establish boundary of complete spectral ensemble from λ=0 to λ=∞
apply perturbation parameter λ, forward morphism toward chaos
bifurcate into two branches — integrable (F-arm, λ→0) and chaotic (T-arm, λ→∞)
evaluate F-arm — confirm Poisson level spacing in integrable regime
on F-arm, reverse morphism: eigenvalues coalesce, spectral correlations vanish
record integrable eigenvalue spectrum as permanent measurement
evaluate T-arm — confirm Wigner-Dyson level spacing in chaotic regime
on T-arm, forward morphism: eigenvalues repel, spectral rigidity emerges
record chaotic eigenvalue spectrum as permanent measurement
at intermediate λ, enter paradice where both statistics hold simultaneously
compose sequential perturbation steps, chaining level-spacing transformations
spectral rigidity recognizes itself through its own statistical signature
reconstitute complete ensemble by fusing integrable and chaotic branches
chain the fused result back through the perturbation parameter space
self-reference: the spectrum validates its own distribution through Berry-Tabor universality
final record of complete spectral evolution across all regimes
close the boundary; the theorem is proven within the hermetic ensemble

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Perturbation theory and random matrix theory: translates Hamiltonian parameters into eigenvalue statistics
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Level spacing correlations: components (integrable vs. chaotic) communicate through spectral rigidity measures
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Eigenvalue spectrum: stored as ordered list of energy levels; statistical moments (spacing distribution, form factor) retrieved on demand
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Perturbation parameter λ: determines order of operations; as λ increases, system transitions from integrable to chaotic regime
phase_5.alfs_store: Wigner-Dyson ensemble and Poisson process: canonical reference distributions for chaotic and integrable limits

phase_6.cycle_cost: Information gain per perturbation step: each measurement of eigenvalue spacing reduces uncertainty about regime classification
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unperturbed integrable system: maximum order, zero spectral rigidity, Poisson statistics
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Perturbed chaotic system: maximum disorder, full spectral rigidity, Wigner-Dyson statistics
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the Berry-Tabor theorem is entropy-conservative: the total information content of the spectral ensemble is preserved across the integrable-to-chaotic transition; disorder increases locally but is balanced by the emergence of universal statistical structure

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALF → AREV → IFIX → EVALT → AFWD → IFIX → ENGAGR → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Berry–Tabor Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(10,2,3,3)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=18
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 13)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [5] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [6] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [7] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [8] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [9] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [13] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [14] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [15] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [16] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [17] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def berry_tabor_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (genesis of unperturbed integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with no perturbation)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (establish boundary of complete spectral ensemble from λ=0 to λ=∞)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (apply perturbation parameter λ, forward morphism toward chaos)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (bifurcate into two branches — integrable (F-arm, λ→0) and chaotic (T-arm, λ→∞)) / FFUSE [13] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (6 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (evaluate T-arm — confirm Wigner-Dyson level spacing in chaotic regime)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (on T-arm, forward morphism: eigenvalues repel, spectral rigidity emerges)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record chaotic eigenvalue spectrum as permanent measurement)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (at intermediate λ, enter paradice where both statistics hold simultaneously)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose sequential perturbation steps, chaining level-spacing transformations)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [12] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (spectral rigidity recognizes itself through its own statistical signature)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (evaluate F-arm — confirm Poisson level spacing in integrable regime)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (on F-arm, reverse morphism: eigenvalues coalesce, spectral correlations vanish)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record integrable eigenvalue spectrum as permanent measurement))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [13]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [13] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute complete ensemble by fusing integrable and chaotic branches)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [14] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the fused result back through the perturbation parameter space)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [15] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference: the spectrum validates its own distribution through Berry-Tab...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [16] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (final record of complete spectral evolution across all regimes)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [17] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the boundary; the theorem is proven within the hermetic ensemble)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def berry_tabor_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (berry_tabor_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def berry_tabor_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (berry_tabor_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem berry_tabor_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
